description_long: This is a two-story suburban house with a red-tiled gable roof and a connected single-car garage. The exterior walls are finished in a light, neutral color, and the house sits on a paved surface that extends to form a driveway and walkways. The front facade features a main entry door with a small overhang, flanked by windows, and the garage door is prominently positioned to one side. The rear of the house includes large glass sliding doors and several windows, providing ample natural light to the interior. The roof is pitched, with the main section running parallel to the longer axis of the house, and a secondary gable over the garage. The window arrangement is asymmetrical, with a mix of standard and larger panes, and the overall design is simple and functional, typical of modern suburban developments.
description: A two-story suburban house with a red-tiled gable roof, attached garage, and light-colored exterior walls, featuring asymmetrical windows and a paved driveway.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry door, garage door, and several windows, with the red-tiled roof and paved driveway clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the house, the sloped roof, and minimal window openings on this facade.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying large glass sliding doors, multiple windows, and the paved area extending behind the house.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the garage side wall, additional windows, and the continuation of the red-tiled roof.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple main door with a small overhang, no porch, and direct access from the paved driveway.
